title: S'more Fun Than You Can Handle!
story: Gooey marshmallows, melted chocolate, and crunchy graham crackers come together for the ultimate campfire treat on National S'mores Day every August 10! People celebrate by roasting marshmallows over fire pits, camp stoves, or even kitchen flames. Some folks get creative with different chocolates like peanut butter cups or cookies instead of graham crackers. Restaurants serve s'mores pizza and ice cream flavors, while families have backyard s'mores parties. title: Roaring for the King of Beasts
story: Lions get their own special day every August 10 when the world celebrates World Lion Day! Zoos around the globe host lion-themed events where kids can learn amazing facts about these powerful cats. Wildlife organizations share incredible photos and videos of lions in Africa on social media. Many people donate money to help protect wild lions and their habitats. title: Spoil Your Dog Day: Paws for Celebration!
story: Dogs everywhere get the royal treatment on August 10 during Spoil Your Dog Day! Pet owners go completely overboard, buying fancy treats, new toys, and even doggy spa visits. title: Fuel Made From French Fries!
story: Cars can run on cooking oil from your favorite restaurant! World Biodiesel Day celebrates this amazing fuel made from used vegetable oil, animal fats, and even algae. title: Fiery Sky Visitor Skims Earth's Edge
story: A blazing fireball lit up the sky in 1972! On August 10, a massive meteor weighing about a million kilograms zoomed toward Earth. title: Blazing Daylight Meteor Streaks Across Sky

This rare celestial spectacle, dubbed 'The Great Fireball,' wowed onlookers across two countries. Eyewitnesses described a brilliant streak of light racing overhead, brighter than the sun itself. story: A carpenter named Ole Kirk Christiansen had a brilliant idea in 1932. He started making wooden toys in his workshop in Billund, Denmark. This was the beginning of the Lego Group! Fender invented iconic electric guitars like the Telecaster and Stratocaster, as well as the Precision Bass. title: Viking Queens Sail into History
story: Shovels struck gold in Norway! On August 10, 1903, archaeologists unearthed a breathtaking Viking ship near Tønsberg. The Oseberg, built around 820 AD, wasn't just any vessel. Hidden in a burial mound, it held the remains of two women, possibly queens, surrounded by 15 horses. This incredible find opened a window into the Viking world, revealing their craftsmanship, beliefs, and the important roles women played in their society.

story: Zoom! On August 10, 1965, test pilot Joe Engle rocketed into the history books. Flying the X-15, a sleek experimental aircraft, Engle pushed the limits of speed and altitude. He soared to an incredible height of 51.3 miles, brushing the edge of space. The X-15 screamed through the sky at a mind-boggling 3,550 miles per hour - that's over five times the speed of sound! title: Space Mail: The First Delivery from Orbit
story: Whoosh! A special capsule zoomed back to Earth, splashing down in the Pacific Ocean. This wasn't just any package - it was the first object ever returned from space! On August 10, 1960, the Discoverer 13 satellite launched into orbit. Its mission? To prove we could send things to space and bring them safely home. title: The Engine That Changed the World

story: Zooming through space, NASA's Magellan spacecraft reached Venus on August 10, 1990. This robotic explorer was named after the famous Portuguese navigator Ferdinand Magellan. Its mission? title: The Man Who Wanted to Save Daylight

title: FDR's Life-Changing Summer Illness
story: Franklin D. Roosevelt's life took a dramatic turn in 1921. While vacationing at his summer home on Campobello Island, Canada, the future president suddenly fell ill. title: Cycling Revolution at the Olympics
story: Pedals whirred and wheels spun as cyclists raced in a brand new way at the 1936 Olympics. For the first time ever, the road race event started with all riders together in one big group. French cyclists dominated, with Robert Charpentier zooming to gold and Guy Lapébie snagging silver. Their speedy teamwork also earned France the gold medal in the team competition on August 10.

title: All Blacks Triumph in Rugby Showdown
story: Rugby fans roared as New Zealand's All Blacks clinched victory in Cape Town on August 10, 1996. They defeated South Africa 29-18, securing the first-ever Tri Nations Rugby Series title. title: Isobel Gunn: Daring Disguise in Canada
story: Isobel Gunn was born in Tankerness, Scotland in 1780. She became a trailblazer by disguising herself as a man to work for the Hudson Bay Company. title: A Hospital That Changed Healthcare
story: Imagine a hospital built just for shipyard workers! In 1942, Henry J. Kaiser did exactly that. He opened the Kaiser Richmond Field Hospital in California on August 10. This special hospital was for employees of Kaiser Shipyards. It was the start of something big - the Kaiser Permanente healthcare model. This new way of providing medical care would go on to help millions of people stay healthy.
